name: Algerian Northern Highlands

staple_foods.name: Sigoise olive

primary_metabolites.oleic_acid: 66-68% of total fatty acids [Sigoise, Setif — Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr]
primary_metabolites.linoleic_acid: 12-13% of total fatty acids [Guerfel et al. 2022]

key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: 560-675 ppm total polyphenols [Sigoise olive, Setif — Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr]

staple_foods.notes: Highland Setif site. Adequate water availability at 450mm precipitation produces moderate polyphenol and tocopherol levels. Oleic acid dominant as typical for Mediterranean olives. Natural experiment pair with RSU-16 (Algerian Saharan Margin) — same Sigoise cultivar under contrasting water stress conditions.
data_sources: Guerfel et al. 2022 Food Sci Nutr PMC9179137 — Sigoise olive oil composition across Algerian regions

staple_foods.name: durum wheat
staple_foods.notes: Durum wheat (Triticum durum) is the foundation starch of Algerian cuisine — used for couscous, bread, and pasta. Algeria is among the world's largest per-capita durum wheat consumers. FDC data needed.

staple_foods.name: lamb

umami_compounds.glutamate: ~0.3 g/100g free glutamate [lamb muscle — food chemistry literature estimate, FDC data needed]

staple_foods.notes: Lamb is the dominant meat protein in highland Algerian cuisine. Sheep herding is well-suited to the limestone highland terrain. FDC data needed.

staple_foods.name: harissa

key_flavor_bioactives.capsaicin: present — key pungency compound of harissa chili base, concentration varies by formulation

staple_foods.notes: Harissa is the defining aromatic condiment of North African cuisine — a paste of hot chili peppers, garlic, olive oil, and spices. Capsaicinoids are the key bioactives. FDC data needed for quantified profile.

staple_foods.name: leben

organic_acids.lactic_acid: 0.5-0.9 g/100g [fermented milk — LAB fermentation literature estimate, FDC data needed]

staple_foods.notes: Leben is a traditional North African fermented milk product, similar to buttermilk or drinkable yogurt. Produced by LAB fermentation of sheep or cow milk. Widely consumed across Algeria. FDC data needed.

notes: Algerian northern highland site (Setif region, ~1000m). Mediterranean climate with limestone parent material. Natural experiment pair with RSU-16 (Algerian Saharan Margin) — same Sigoise olive cultivar under contrasting aridity. Highland conditions (adequate rainfall, moderate temperature) produce lower polyphenol and tocopherol concentrations in olive oil than the water-stressed Saharan margin. Durum wheat and lamb represent the dominant starch-protein axis of Algerian highland cuisine.
